zh.Bolstaholm, Geta, Åland, Turku ja Pori,: 芬蘭圖爾庫 亞 波里奧藍德塔波爾斯塔霍爾姆

zh.Bolstaholm, Geta, Ahvenanmaa, Turku ja P: 芬蘭圖爾庫 亞 波里奧藍德塔波爾斯塔霍爾姆
zh.lifeSummary: Margareta till Dalkarby於大約1270年出生在芬蘭圖爾庫 亞 波里奧藍德喬馬拉達爾卡比。她和Andris Andrisson af Bolstaholm至少有3個兒子。她去世於1330年之後在芬蘭圖爾庫 亞 波里奧藍德塔波爾斯塔霍爾姆。

nameMeanings.name: till
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Till, a pet form of Old French Matild(e) (in its Latin form Matilda), which was an alternative to Maud; see Maude 2. A diminutive form occurs in Tillotson .

variants.name: Tilly
meanings: German: from a shortened form of Tilling, which itself is a patronymic from Till .
English and Irish: variant of   Tilley  .

variants.name: Tyl
meanings: Polish and Czech: from the personal name Tyl, a Slavicized form of German Till and Thiel 1. The same Sorbian surname, which may also be from a short form of the personal name Bartyl ‘Bartholomew’ (see Barthel ), is found only in a Germanized form, Thiel 2.

variants.name: Maude
meanings: habitational name from Mold in Flintshire, Wales, which name derives from Anglo-Norman French mont haut ‘high hill’ (Latinised as mons altus), the Norman name for Bailey Hill on which the castle stood. The name de Montalt, de Mohaut was borne by an Anglo-Norman aristocratic family who acted as castle wardens there but who had their chief seat in Riddlesden in Yorkshire, where the surname proliferated as Mawhood and Maude. In the 17th century one branch of the family acquired lands in Ireland. The Maude baronetcy of Dundrum, Tipperary, was created in 1705 for Robert Maude. His son Cornwallis Maude, Baron de Montalt, was created first Viscount Hawarden in 1793. Mowatt is the Scottish form of the name, from a different branch of the same family.

occasionally from Middle English and Norman French female personal name Mahald or Maud, which are Old French forms of ancient Germanic Mahthild (Latinised as Matilda), composed of the Germanic elements maht ‘might, power’ + hild ‘battle’. This personal name partly owed its great popularity among Normans in England to the fact that it was the name of the wife (c. 1031–83) of William the Conqueror. Surnames derived from this personal name are found in many forms and spellings, for example, Mould and Moll .

variants.name: Tillmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Ruedi, Franz, Georg, Helmut, Joerg.  Scandinavian Holger, Johan.
North German: elaborated form of Till , with the addition of -mann ‘man’.

variants.name: Tillett
meanings: English (East Anglia): from the Middle English female personal name Tillot, a diminutive of Till , itself a pet form of Matill (Matilda). Compare Tillotson .

variants.name: Tillis
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of Tills, itself a variant of Till , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Margareta
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaret
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margrethe
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretha
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

en.lifeSummary: Margareta till Dalkarby was born about 1270, in Dalkarby, Jomala, Åland, Turku ja Pori, Finland. She had at least 3 sons with Andris Andrisson af Bolstaholm. She died after 1330, in Bolstaholm, Geta, Åland, Turku ja Pori, Finland.

ja.lifeSummary: Margareta till Dalkarbyは約1270年にフィンランドトゥルク ジャ ポリオーランド諸島ジョマラダルカルビーで生まれた。彼女はAndris Andrisson af Bolstaholmとの間に少なくとも3人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1330年以後にフィンランドトゥルク ジャ ポリオーランド諸島ジェタボルスタホルムで死去した。

ko.Bolstaholm, Geta, Åland, Turku ja Pori,: 핀란드 터르쿠 자 포리 a랜드 짓아 볼스타홀름
ko.Dalkarby, Jomala, Åland, Åbo och Björneb: 핀란드 터르쿠 자 포리 a랜드 조마라 다카르비
ko.Bolstaholm, Geta, Ahvenanmaa, Turku ja P: 핀란드 터르쿠 자 포리 a랜드 짓아 볼스타홀름
ko.lifeSummary: Margareta till Dalkarby(은)는 대략 1270년 핀란드 터르쿠 자 포리 a랜드 조마라 다카르비에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Andris Andrisson af Bolstaholm(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1330년 이후 핀란드 터르쿠 자 포리 a랜드 짓아 볼스타홀름에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Margareta till Dalkarby родилась около 1270 г. в местности далкарби, джомала, Аландские острова, турку джа пори, Финляндия. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына от Andris Andrisson af Bolstaholm. Она умерла после 1330 г. в местности болстахолм, джета, Аландские острова, турку джа пори, Финляндия.
